message:
feat(indexer): expose protected-retention generation IDs + retained byte counter on cleanup dry-run

`LexicalCleanupDryRunPlan` now reports, alongside the existing
reclaim/retain breakdowns, a dedicated view of generations whose
retention is "protected" — i.e. the inventory (or one of its
shards) falls into a disposition whose retained bytes are not up
for reclamation on any cleanup pass: CurrentPublished, ActiveWork,
or QuarantinedRetained.

Fields added to the plan:

  - `protected_generation_ids: Vec<String>` — generation IDs whose
    inventory or any shard reports a protected disposition with a
    non-zero `retained_bytes`. Order reflects first-seen.
  - `protected_retained_bytes: u64` — cumulative retained bytes
    attributable to those protected dispositions, accumulated with
    saturating addition. If per-shard protected bytes are seen we
    sum those; otherwise the inventory-level `retained_bytes`
    contributes once (so we don't double-count a top-level
    retention that isn't shard-scoped).

Both fields are included in the plan's stable hash so the
determinism contract across runs covers the new surface, and the
JSON shape exposes them under `protected_retained_bytes` and
`protected_generation_ids`. The new field on `protected_generation_ids`
carries `serde(default)` so older tooling that reads the JSON
doesn't break, but the hash/test assertions in this commit pin the
new contract going forward.

`is_protected_retention` is a small private helper that names the
disposition set in one place so the rule can evolve cleanly.

Two existing unit tests in the module were extended to assert
both the numeric counter and the generation-id list for the new
surface, including the JSON serialization shape.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
